data.description: Nearby allied units gain +25% damage for 8 seconds.
data.name: Rally Banner

data.description: Base hub that trains Workers only, anchors the camp, and researches core camp upgrades. Losing it means defeat.
data.name: Command Hall

data.description: Army production building for Militia, Rangers, and basic troop research.
data.name: Barracks

data.description: Destroy this to win the skirmish.
data.name: Enemy Stronghold

data.description: Enemy production structure.
data.name: Enemy Barracks

data.counterplay: Protect sites with Militia screens and pull fragile units back before raids land.
data.name: Raider
data.shortDescription: Pressures resource sites, assigned Workers, and exposed economy routes.
data.threatWarning: Raiders will look for exposed Workers and resource sites.

data.counterplay: Keep hero and Retinue near army cover; avoid isolated dives into enemy space.
data.name: Hunter
data.shortDescription: Sends escorted pressure toward the hero or deployed Retinue when they are exposed.
data.threatWarning: Hunter squads may probe your hero or Retinue when escorted.

data.counterplay: Regroup before the late push and use one Retinue reinforcement at a safe timing.
data.name: Warband
data.shortDescription: Builds toward a late mixed-unit push without increasing early spam.
data.threatWarning: A mixed Warband can form later if the fight drags on.

data.description: A measured ranged spell that punishes exposed troops.
data.name: Hexfire Bolt

data.description: A fortress order that briefly reinforces nearby defenders.
data.name: Hold the Line

data.flavorText: Veyra reads the smoke around the Aether Well and punishes armies that cross the ford unsupported.
data.name: Veyra of the Cinders
data.title: Hexfire Seer

behaviorNotes: Frontline is thinner than other profiles.
data.description: A caster-heavy profile that fields more Hexers, fewer melee blockers, and dangerous burst waves after the opening.
data.name: Hexfire Cult

data.fantasy: Salto militias, scouts, and oathbound mystics defending the Barrosan Marches.

mechanics.economyStyle: Balanced site economy with strong campaign support from trusted local communities.
mechanics.magicStyle: Supportive Acolytes and hero-led blessings rather than raw burst damage.
mechanics.militaryStyle: Reliable infantry, Rangers, defensive Watchtowers, and leadership scaling.

data.name: Barrosan Freeholds

data.fantasy: A ruthless warband culture built around raiders, hex-magic, and brutal champions.

mechanics.economyStyle: Aggressive low-cost early pressure with less interest in defensive staying power.

factionModifiers.description: Hexers apply a short burn that deals damage over time.
factionModifiers.name: Hexfire Burn

factionModifiers.description: Ashen units deal more damage while below half health.
factionModifiers.name: Ashen Fury

factionModifiers.description: Ashen attack waves are trained for faster forward pressure.
factionModifiers.name: Smoke March

mechanics.magicStyle: Hexfire pressure applies burn damage and punishes clumped or wounded troops.
mechanics.militaryStyle: Cheap Raiders, hard-hitting waves, fragile casters, and Brutes as the durable exception.

data.name: Ashen Covenant

data.description: A strong melee commander who makes nearby soldiers hit harder.
data.name: Warlord

data.description: A magic damage specialist who burns priority targets.
data.name: Arcanist

data.description: A healer and holy commander who keeps a small force alive.
data.name: Shepherd

data.battleLocalCopy: Linked Ward is battle-local and does not change campaign saves.

benefit.name: Linked Ward
benefit.summary: Friendly units and buildings near active linked sites take 8% less incoming damage.

data.briefingCopy: Hold two linked sites to wake a Lume Ward. Enemy recapture severs the link.
data.counterplay: Enemy recapture severs the link; retake both endpoints to restore it.
data.hudObjective: Hold linked sites to wake a Lume Ward.

links.displayName: West Stone Cut to Ford Toll
Ford Toll to North Aether Spring

data.description: A ruined ford splits two attack lanes around a dangerous center, with safer but slower resource routes on the flanks.
data.name: Broken Ford
strategicNotes: The central ford is valuable and exposed; claiming it early risks fighting neutral monsters and enemy patrols.
Side resources are easier to hold, but their slower income rewards patient expansion.
Blocked ruins and water pockets make army staging and base exits feel tighter than First Claim.

data.name: Control
data.objectiveHint: Capture and defend resource sites before committing to the final push.
data.shortDescription: Contest the map economy and hold resource sites long enough to outpace the enemy.

data.description: A ruined well on Broken Ford pulls the fight toward site control, Worker assignment, and Lume pressure.

missionBriefing.afterActionSummary: The well is guarded again, and the chapel route is exposed.
missionBriefing.primaryObjective: Destroy the Ashen base after contesting the ruined well.
missionBriefing.recommendedBuildHint: Seer mana and cooldown support fit the longer aether fight.
missionBriefing.rewardPreview: First clear: XP, Aether, Stone, and the Aether Lens.
missionBriefing.summary: A tighter control mission around the ruined well, assigned Workers, and the Lume Surge.

data.name: Aether Well Ruins

data.description: Raised for command, stripped of title, and hungry for a second banner.
data.name: Exiled Noble

data.description: Currency used for units, buildings, and campaign payments.
data.name: Crowns

data.description: Building material used for halls, towers, and lodges.
data.name: Stone

data.description: Military material used for weapons and armored troops.
data.name: Iron

data.description: Magic resource used for spells, rituals, and arcane units.
data.name: Aether

data.afterActionSummary: Defensive plan used; Retinue reinforcement support was available for the fight.
data.description: Default safe plan: protect Workers, sites, hero, and Retinue before committing.
data.effectSummary: Call Retinue costs 60 Crowns instead of 75 in eligible campaign battles.
data.hudSummary: Guarded Advance active: Retinue call costs less.
data.name: Guarded Advance
data.recommendedCounterplay: Use this when raids, hero probes, or late pushes threaten exposed units.

data.afterActionSummary: Economy plan used; early resource support helped the opening build.
data.description: Economy opener: capture and upgrade sites earlier, then convert resources into army.
data.effectSummary: Start with +35 Crowns and +20 Stone in eligible campaign battles.
data.hudSummary: Resource Push active: extra Crowns and Stone at start.
data.name: Resource Push
data.recommendedCounterplay: Use this into Fortress defenses or when you want more site-control tempo.

data.label: Utility / Site Support
data.summary: Builder and economy support. Workers are useful in combat only as a last resort.
data.tacticalHint: Use Workers to build, repair, finish construction, and boost captured resource sites.

data.label: Frontline / Melee
data.summary: Durable enough to stand in front and hold enemies away from fragile units.
data.tacticalHint: Put Militia in front, use Hold Ground when defending, and let them screen Rangers or Acolytes.

data.label: Ranged / Focus Fire
data.summary: Longer-ranged damage that needs space and frontline cover.
data.tacticalHint: Focus Rangers on priority targets from behind Militia; move them if enemies close the gap.

data.label: Enemy Pressure / Melee
data.summary: Fast melee pressure that contests workers, sites, and exposed ranged units.
data.tacticalHint: Screen with Militia and avoid letting Raiders reach fragile backline units.

data.label: Enemy Aether / Ranged
data.summary: Fragile ranged caster that punishes clumped or wounded units.
data.tacticalHint: Focus fire Hexers before they chip down grouped troops.

data.label: Enemy Frontline / Heavy
data.summary: Slow heavy melee unit built to soak damage during enemy pushes.
data.tacticalHint: Kite Brutes with range and do not let them tie up fragile units for long.

data.label: Enemy Commander / Champion
data.summary: Rival battlefield leader that turns larger enemy attacks into a serious threat.
data.tacticalHint: Defeating the commander is a major objective and can unlock the relic reward flow.

data.description: A camp hand who can establish basic military buildings.
data.name: Worker

data.description: Reliable town guard with spears and stubborn courage.
data.name: Militia

data.description: A light archer who fights best behind a front line.
data.name: Ranger

data.description: Fast enemy infantry that pressures resource sites.
data.name: Raider

data.description: A brittle caster whose bolts punish clumped troops.
data.name: Hexer

data.description: A heavy brawler meant to soak damage during attack waves.
data.name: Brute

data.description: A dangerous commander who joins larger enemy attacks.
data.name: Ashen Commander
